2	\chapter{Weil divisors on a smooth proper curve (RR.1)}
3	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}
4	% SOURCE: Hartshorne, \emph{Algebraic Geometry}, II \S6, pp.~130--137 +
5	% IV.1, pp.~294--296 (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf).
6	% Stacks pointers (tag-level, not verbatim-quoted): 02RW (divisors),
7	% 02ME (order at a point on a regular 1-dimensional scheme),
8	% 0BE0 (degree of a divisor on a curve), 0BE3 (principal divisors have
9	% degree zero on a complete nonsingular curve).
10	\section{Setup and motivation}
11	This chapter is the first of four sub-build chapters \texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4}
12	that close the project's headline \emph{RR bridge}
13	\[
14	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
15	  \,\colon\,
16	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
17	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}
18	\]
19	The bridge as proved in Hartshorne (Example~IV.1.3.5) chains together:
20	divisor of a closed point on a curve (\texttt{RR.1}, this chapter); the
21	Riemann--Roch dimension formula \(\ell(D) = \deg D + 1\) for \(\deg D \geq 0\) on
22	a genus-\(0\) curve (\texttt{RR.2}); the global-sections argument identifying
23	\(\dim_k H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(P)) = 2\) via \(1\) and a non-constant function
24	(\texttt{RR.3}); the ``rational curve \(\Rightarrow \cong \mathbb P^1\)''
25	classification recovered as a degree-\(1\) morphism into \(\mathbb P^1\) via
26	\texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} (\texttt{RR.4}).
27	Mathlib ships no \texttt{WeilDivisor} on a scheme. Adjacent pieces exist, but
28	none of them is the data structure we need: \texttt{MeromorphicOn.divisor}
29	(\texttt{Mathlib.Analysis.Meromorphic.Divisor}) is the order function of a
30	meromorphic function valued in a \emph{normed} field, not a formal sum of
31	codimension-\(1\) cycles on a scheme; \texttt{CommRing.Pic}
32	(\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}) is the ring-level Picard group of
33	invertible modules under tensor product, with no scheme-level analogue;
34	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap}
35	(\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap}) packages rational
36	maps but not divisors. Consequently this chapter, and the three siblings, are
37	\emph{project-bespoke}: the only Mathlib tools they rely on are
38	discrete-valuation rings, the function field, and the ambient scheme API.
39	The audit grounding this scope decision is in
40	\texttt{analogies/rrbridge-survey.md}.
41	This chapter establishes the formal-sum data type
42	\(\mathrm{Div}(X) = \bigoplus_{\substack{Z \subset X \\ \mathrm{codim} 1}} \mathbb Z\) on a
43	Noetherian integral scheme \(X\) satisfying Hartshorne's condition \((*)\), the
44	principal-divisor homomorphism \(\mathrm{div} \colon K(X)^{\times} \to \mathrm{Div}(X)\) on a
45	curve, the degree map \(\deg \colon \mathrm{Div}(C) \to \mathbb Z\) on a smooth proper
46	curve over an algebraically closed field, and the linear-equivalence
47	relation. The \emph{degree-zero of a principal divisor} statement
48	(\ref{thm:principal_deg_zero}) is included for completeness; its proof
49	decomposes into a separate sub-build inside \texttt{RR.1} (it uses the
50	auxiliary lemma that any non-constant rational function on \(C\) defines a
51	finite morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1\)). The line-bundle construction $\mathcal
52	O_C(D)\( and the associated cohomology computation \)H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))$
53	live in the sibling chapter \texttt{RR.3}; the Riemann--Roch dimension
54	formula lives in \texttt{RR.2}; the genus-\(0\) classification proper lives in
55	\texttt{RR.4}.
56	Throughout this chapter, the standing convention is Hartshorne's condition
57	\((*)\):
58	% SOURCE QUOTE: "\((\ast)\) \(X\) is a noetherian integral separated scheme which
59	% is regular in codimension one."
60	\textit{Source: Hartshorne, II \S6, p.~130, the conditional \((*)\).}
61	\[
62	  (\ast)\qquad
63	  X \text{ is a noetherian integral separated scheme which is regular in
64	  codimension one.}
65	\]
66	For the degree map and for principal-divisor statements on a curve, we
67	additionally assume \(X\) is a complete (i.e.\ proper) nonsingular curve over
68	an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), where Hartshorne's condition \((*)\) is
69	automatic.
70	\paragraph{Standing hypothesis \((*)\) in the Lean encoding (iter-173 pin).}
71	Mathlib (as of \texttt{b80f227}) does not bundle Hartshorne's four-clause
72	\((\ast)\) as a single typeclass. The chapter pins \((*)\) to a \emph{layered}
73	typeclass set that the prover-agent threads through the Lean signatures in
74	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean}:
75	\begin{itemize}
76	  \item \textbf{Basic formal-sum layer} (carrier of \ref{def:prime_divisor} and
77	    \ref{def:codim1_cycles}, the additive group \(\mathrm{Div}(X)\), the degree
78	    \ref{def:divisor_degree}, the degree-homomorphism
79	    \ref{thm:divisor_degree_hom}, and \ref{def:divisor_closed_point}): the
80	    only Mathlib typeclass invoked is \texttt{[IsIntegral X]}
81	    (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme} via
82	    \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Properties}). Integrality of \(X\) already
83	    delivers \texttt{IrreducibleSpace X.carrier} (the carrier-level instance
84	    chain), so \(X\) has a unique generic point and the function field
85	    \texttt{X.functionField} (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.FunctionField})
86	    is a field; separatedness and noetherianness are \emph{not} required for
87	    the bare formal-sum data type.
88	  \item \textbf{Order/principal layer} (\ref{def:order_at_point},
89	    \ref{def:principal_divisor}, \ref{thm:principal_hom}): the Lean
90	    signature additionally threads
91	    \texttt{[IsLocallyNoetherian X]}
92	    (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Noetherian}) and Hartshorne's
93	    regular-in-codimension-one clause. The latter has \emph{no} Mathlib
94	    typeclass shipping it directly; the chapter's pin is the project-side
95	    predicate ``for every \(x \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} = 1\) the
96	    stalk \(\mathcal O_{X,x}\) is a discrete valuation ring'', encodable as
97	    the conjunction
98	    $\forall x,\; \texttt{Order.coheight}\,x = 1 \Rightarrow
99	    \texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing}\,(\mathcal O_{X,x})$. Iter-173 introduced
100	    the \texttt{Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne} class

240	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II \S6, p.~130, the fourth clause of \((\ast)\)
241	  % (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 147).
242	  % Stacks pointers: tag 0BX5 (regular schemes), tag 02ME
243	  % (DVR-of-stalk characterisation at codim-1 points).
244	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\((\ast)\) \(X\) is a noetherian integral separated scheme
245	  % which is regular in codimension one."
246	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II \S6, p.~130 (the fourth clause of \((\ast)\)).}
247	  A scheme \(X\) is \emph{regular in codimension one} when for every point
248	  \(x \in X\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} = 1\) (equivalently,
249	  \(\texttt{Order.coheight}\,x = 1\)) the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X,x}\) is
250	  a discrete valuation ring. The Lean encoding bundles this conjunction as
251	  a one-field \texttt{Prop} class so that downstream order / principal /
252	  degree lemmas may consume it as a typeclass hypothesis.
253	\end{definition}
254	\section{Open-immersion descent for prime divisors}
255	\label{sec:open_immersion_descent}
256	\textit{Source: Stacks Project tags 02IZ (stalk under open immersion) +
257	01J6 (open immersion preserves regularity); cross-referenced with the
258	project-local iter-183 substrate
259	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CoheightBridge.lean}.}
260	
261	The chapter's order / principal-divisor API is naturally stated on the
262	ambient scheme \(X\), but several downstream proofs (the
263	\(\texttt{rationalMap\_order\_finite\_support}\) non-zero branch in
264	particular) require localising to an affine open \(U \hookrightarrow X\),
265	identifying prime divisors of \(X\) lying in \(U\) with prime divisors of
266	\(U\), and transporting stalk-level data along the open-immersion stalk
267	isomorphism. This section records the substrate bijection
268	\(\{Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor} \mid Y.\texttt{point} \in U\}
269	 \;\simeq\; U.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\)
270	and the open-immersion descent of the \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\)
271	class.
272	
273	\begin{definition}
274	[Restriction of a prime divisor to an open subscheme]
275	  \label{def:primeDivisor_restrictToOpen}
276	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen}
277	  \uses{def:prime_divisor}
278	  Given a scheme \(X\), an open subscheme \(U \hookrightarrow X\), a prime
279	  divisor \(Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\), and a witness \(Y.\texttt{point} \in U\),
280	  the restriction \(Y |_U : U.\texttt{toScheme}.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) packages
281	  the point \(Y.\texttt{point}\) (viewed as a point of \(U\) via the open
282	  inclusion) with the coheight witness from
283	  \(\texttt{Order.coheight\_eq\_of\_isOpenEmbedding}\) (project-local
284	  substrate, iter-183 \texttt{CoheightBridge.lean} L175--L185).
285	\end{definition}
286	\begin{definition}
287	[Push of a prime divisor along an open immersion]
288	  \label{def:primeDivisor_ofOpen}
289	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ofOpen}
290	  \uses{def:prime_divisor}
291	  Given a scheme \(X\), an open subscheme \(U \hookrightarrow X\), and a
292	  prime divisor \(Y : U.\texttt{toScheme}.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\), the push
293	  \(\texttt{ofOpen}\,U\,Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) packages the point
294	  \(Y.\texttt{point} \in U \hookrightarrow X\) (viewed as a point of \(X\))
295	  with the coheight witness from
296	  \(\texttt{Order.coheight\_eq\_of\_isOpenEmbedding}\) applied in the
297	  opposite direction.
298	\end{definition}
299	\begin{lemma}
300	[Bijection of prime divisors under open immersion]
301	  \label{lem:primeDivisor_equivOpen}
302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.equivOpen}
303	  \uses{def:primeDivisor_restrictToOpen, def:primeDivisor_ofOpen}
304	  For an open subscheme \(U \hookrightarrow X\) of a scheme \(X\), there is a
305	  bijection
306	  \[
307	    \{Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor} \mid Y.\texttt{point} \in U\}
308	    \;\simeq\;
309	    U.\texttt{toScheme}.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}
310	  \]
311	  packaging the restriction (\ref{def:primeDivisor_restrictToOpen}) and the
312	  push (\ref{def:primeDivisor_ofOpen}) as mutual inverses. Both
313	  \texttt{left\_inv} and \texttt{right\_inv} hold definitionally
314	  (\texttt{rfl}) because both directions preserve the underlying point
315	  field via subtype eta + structure eta.
316	\end{lemma}
317	\begin{lemma}
318	[Stalk isomorphism across an open immersion]
319	  \label{lem:primeDivisor_stalkIso}
320	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso}
321	  \uses{def:primeDivisor_restrictToOpen}
322	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 02IZ (stalk is invariant under restriction
323	  % to a non-empty open neighbourhood) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex).
324	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, tag 02IZ, plus Mathlib's
325	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.stalkIso}.}
326	  For \(Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) with \(Y.\texttt{point} \in U\), the
327	  stalk presheaf of \(U.\texttt{toScheme}\) at the restricted point coincides,
328	  up to canonical isomorphism, with the stalk presheaf of \(X\) at
329	  \(Y.\texttt{point}\). The Lean wrapper packages Mathlib's
330	  \(\texttt{Scheme.Opens.stalkIso}\) for the project's
331	  \texttt{PrimeDivisor} bundle.
332	\end{lemma}
333	\begin{theorem}
334	[\(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\) descends along open immersion]
335	  \label{thm:isRegularInCodimensionOne_open}
336	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instOpen}
337	  \uses{def:isRegularInCodimensionOne, lem:primeDivisor_stalkIso,
338	    def:primeDivisor_ofOpen}
339	  Let \(X\) be a scheme satisfying \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\)
340	  and \(U \hookrightarrow X\) an open subscheme with
341	  \(\texttt{IsIntegral}\,U.\texttt{toScheme}\) (the latter is \emph{not}
342	  automatic from integrality of \(X\); it is threaded explicitly).
343	  Then \(U.\texttt{toScheme}\) also satisfies
344	  \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\); the DVR-of-stalk property at a
345	  codim-1 point \(Y : U.\texttt{toScheme}.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) is
346	  transported from the corresponding point
347	  \(\texttt{ofOpen}\,U\,Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) on the ambient
348	  scheme via the stalk isomorphism (\ref{lem:primeDivisor_stalkIso})
349	  together with
350	  \(\texttt{IsDiscreteValuationRing.RingEquivClass.isDiscreteValuationRing}\)
351	  on the ring-equiv image.
352	\end{theorem}
353	\paragraph{Lean-side note.} The 8 axiom-clean iter-200 substrate
354	declarations
355	(\(\texttt{PrimeDivisor.ext}\), \(\texttt{restrictToOpen}\),
356	\(\texttt{ofOpen}\), the two \(\texttt{@[simp]}\) field-extraction
357	helpers \(\texttt{restrictToOpen\_point}\) /
358	\(\texttt{ofOpen\_point}\), \(\texttt{equivOpen}\),
359	\(\texttt{stalkIso}\), and the descent instance
360	\(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne.instOpen}\)) consume the project-local
361	substrate \(\texttt{Order.coheight\_eq\_of\_isOpenEmbedding}\)
362	(\texttt{CoheightBridge.lean} L175--L185) and Mathlib's
363	\(\texttt{Scheme.Opens.stalkIso}\). Realised cost: \(+120\) LOC over
364	\texttt{WeilDivisor.lean} L153--L305 (iter-200).
365	
366	\paragraph{End-to-end map: Sub-builds 1--3 \(\Rightarrow\) closure of
367	\(\texttt{rationalMap\_order\_finite\_support}\) (\ref{lem:rationalMap_order_finite_support}, non-zero branch L535).}
368	The L535 closure target is: for an integral, locally-Noetherian scheme
369	\(X\) satisfying \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\) and a non-zero
370	\(f \in X.\texttt{functionField}\),
371	\((\texttt{Function.support}\,(Y \mapsto \texttt{order}\,Y\,f)).\texttt{Finite}\).
372	Hartshorne II.6.1 / Stacks 02RV proves this by reducing to an
373	affine open neighbourhood and citing height-1 prime decomposition
374	on a Noetherian ring. The project decomposes the proof into three
375	project-local Sub-builds plus a terminal closure step:
376	
377	\begin{itemize}
378	  \item \textbf{Sub-build 1 (CLOSED iter-200, 8 axiom-clean decls
379	    L153--L305).} Open-immersion descent of the
380	    \(\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) bundle: bijection between
381	    prime divisors of \(X\) lying in an open \(U\) and prime divisors
382	    of \(U.\texttt{toScheme}\); stalk-isomorphism wrapper around
383	    Mathlib's \(\texttt{Scheme.Opens.stalkIso}\); descent of the
384	    \(\texttt{IsRegularInCodimensionOne}\) class.
385	  \item \textbf{Sub-build 2 (iter-201 target, \(\sim 30\)--\(50\) LOC).}
386	    Naturality of \(\texttt{Ring.ordFrac}\) across a ring isomorphism
387	    \(R \simeq S\) of Noetherian-domain stalks of Krull dim
388	    \(\leq 1\), with compatible fraction-field iso \(K_R \simeq K_S\).
389	    Build via Mathlib's \(\texttt{IsFractionRing.ringEquivOfRingEquiv}\)
390	    + naturality of \(\texttt{Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom}\) at the
391	    base ring.
392	  \item \textbf{Sub-build 3 (iter-202 target, \(\sim 30\)--\(50\) LOC).}
393	    Naturality of \(\texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.order}\) across the
394	    stalk iso of Sub-build 1: for
395	    \(Y : X.\texttt{PrimeDivisor}\) with \(Y.\texttt{point} \in U\)
396	    and \(f \in X.\texttt{functionField}\),
397	    \(\texttt{order}\,Y\,f
398	     = \texttt{order}\,(\texttt{restrictToOpen}\,U\,Y\,h)\,f'\)
399	    where \(f'\) is the image of \(f\) under
400	    \(X.\texttt{functionField} \simeq U.\texttt{toScheme}.\texttt{functionField}\)
401	    (Mathlib provides this iso for integral \(X\) via the irreducibility
402	    of the generic point lying in any non-empty open). Direct consumer
403	    of Sub-build 2.
404	  \item \textbf{Terminal closure (iter-203 target, \(\sim 40\)--\(80\) LOC).}
405	    Given a non-zero \(f \in X.\texttt{functionField}\):
406	    (a) reduce to a finite affine cover \(X = \bigcup_{i = 1}^N U_i\)
407	    (Hartshorne II.6 hypothesis; project-side
408	    \texttt{[IsLocallyNoetherian X] + [CompactSpace X]} or just
409	    \texttt{[IsNoetherian X]}); (b) by Sub-build 3, every
410	    \(Y \in \texttt{support}\) lies in some \(U_i\) and has
411	    \(\texttt{order}\,Y\,f = \texttt{order}\,(\texttt{restrictToOpen}\,
412	     U_i\,Y\,h)\,f_i\) for \(f_i\) the corresponding fraction-field
413	    element on \(U_i\); (c) each \(U_i\) is affine Noetherian, so the
414	    height-1 prime decomposition of \(\mathrm{div}(f_i)\) in
415	    \(\Gamma(U_i, \mathcal O)\) is finite by Mathlib's
416	    \(\texttt{Ideal.finite\_minimalPrimes\_of\_isNoetherianRing}\)
417	    applied to the principal ideal \((f_i)\); (d) bound
418	    \(|\texttt{support}|\) by the sum of the finitely many
419	    \(|\texttt{support of div}(f_i)|\) terms; (e) conclude finiteness.
420	\end{itemize}
421	
422	The chain terminates: each Sub-build is bounded in scope to a
423	specific project-local naturality / descent step; Sub-build 3 is a
424	direct consumer of Sub-build 2 with no further branching; the
425	terminal closure is bounded by the cardinality of a finite affine
426	cover. The structural blocker around the
427	\(\texttt{[IsLocallyNoetherian X]}\) vs.\
428	\(\texttt{[IsNoetherian X]}\) hypothesis gap (consumer-propagation
429	USER-blocked, see \texttt{STRATEGY.md} A.4.a row) does \emph{not}
430	affect Sub-build 2 or Sub-build 3, which are stated on the open
431	chart \(U.\texttt{toScheme}\) and consume only local hypotheses; the
432	terminal closure step is where the hypothesis-strength choice
433	becomes load-bearing.
434	\section{Order of a rational function at a prime divisor}
435	\begin{definition}
436	\leanok
437	[Order of a rational function at a prime divisor]
438	  \label{def:order_at_point}
439	  
440	  \uses{def:codim1_cycles}
441	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.order}
442	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, p.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) of a prime
443	  % divisor; "zero/pole of \(f\) along \(Y\)") (read from
444	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
445	  % Stacks pointer: tag 02ME.
446	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its
447	  % generic point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at
448	  % \(\eta\) is a discrete valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the
449	  % function field of \(X\). We call the corresponding discrete valuation
450	  % \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
451	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function
452	  % on \(X\). Then \(v_Y(f)\) is an integer. If it is positive, we say \(f\) has
453	  % a \emph{zero} along \(Y\), of that order; if it is negative, we say \(f\)
454	  % has a \emph{pole} along \(Y\), of order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
455	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\)).}
456	  Let \(X\) satisfy \((*)\). For a prime divisor \(Y \subset X\) with generic
457	  point \(\eta \in Y\), the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
458	  valuation ring (by regularity in codimension one) with quotient field the
459	  function field \(K(X)\). For a nonzero rational function \(f \in K(X)^{\times}\)
460	  the \emph{order of \(f\) along \(Y\)} is the integer
461	  \[
462	    \ord_Y(f) \;:=\; v_Y(f) \;\in\; \mathbb Z,
463	  \]
464	  where \(v_Y\) is the normalised discrete valuation associated to
465	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\). If \(\ord_Y(f) > 0\), \(f\) has a \emph{zero of
466	  order \(\ord_Y(f)\) along \(Y\)}; if \(\ord_Y(f) < 0\), \(f\) has a \emph{pole
467	  of order \(-\ord_Y(f)\) along \(Y\)}.
468	  Specialisation to a curve. If \(C\) is a smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\)
469	  and \(P \in C\) is a closed point, then \(P\) is automatically a prime
470	  divisor (it is closed, integral, of codimension one in the \(1\)-dimensional
471	  scheme \(C\)); the local ring \(\mathcal O_{C, P}\) is a discrete valuation
472	  ring by smoothness, and \(\ord_P(f) = v_P(f)\) is the standard DVR
473	  valuation of \(f\) at \(P\).
474	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope (iter-175 pin, per analogist
475	  \texttt{dvr-rationalmap-order}).} The iter-175 Lean signature is
476	  \begin{quote}
477	    \texttt{noncomputable def order \{X : Scheme.\{u\}\} [IsIntegral X]\\
478	    \phantom{}\quad[IsLocallyNoetherian X] (Y : X.PrimeDivisor)\\
479	    \phantom{}\quad[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]\\
480	    \phantom{}\quad(f : X.functionField) : \(\mathbb Z\)}
481	  \end{quote}
482	  with \emph{no} \(f \neq 0\) hypothesis (junk-on-\(f = 0\) convention,
483	  see below). The body is
484	  \[
485	    \texttt{order}\ Y\ f \;:=\;
486	      \texttt{WithZero.log}\ \bigl(
487	        \texttt{Ring.ordFrac}\ (X.\texttt{presheaf.stalk}\ Y.\texttt{point})\ f
488	      \bigr).
489	  \]
490	  \emph{Mathlib API path.}
491	  \begin{itemize}
492	    \item \texttt{Ring.ordFrac R : K \(\to\!\!*_0\) \(\mathbb Z^{m_0}\)}
493	      (with \(\mathbb Z^{m_0} = \texttt{WithZero (Multiplicative}\ \mathbb Z)\)),
494	      from \texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic} (Stacks
495	      tag~\texttt{02MD}), is Mathlib's canonical monoid-with-zero hom
496	      extending the length-of-quotient valuation
497	      \texttt{Ring.ord} from a Noetherian Krull-dim-\(\leq 1\) ring to
498	      its fraction field via \texttt{Submonoid.LocalizationMap.lift\textsubscript{0}}.
499	      For a DVR-uniformised local ring its value on \(r \in R\) is the
500	      length of \(R/(r)\) (the DVR valuation); on \(a/b \in K\), it is
501	      \(\texttt{ord}(a) - \texttt{ord}(b)\). The hypotheses
502	      \texttt{[CommRing R] [Nontrivial R] [IsNoetherianRing R]\\
503	      [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 R]} are exactly the typeclasses the body
504	      synthesises from \([\texttt{IsIntegral}\ X]\) (delivers
505	      \texttt{IsDomain} hence \texttt{Nontrivial} on the stalk via
506	      \texttt{instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral},
507	      and \texttt{IsFractionRing (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)
508	      X.functionField} via
509	      \texttt{instIsFractionRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafFunctionField}),
510	      from \([\texttt{IsLocallyNoetherian}\ X]\) (delivers
511	      \texttt{IsNoetherianRing} on the stalk via
512	      \texttt{instIsNoetherianRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsLocallyNoetherian}),
513	      and from the explicitly threaded
514	      \texttt{[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]}.
515	    \item \texttt{WithZero.log : \(\mathbb Z^{m_0} \to \mathbb Z\)}
516	      from \texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.GroupWithZero.WithZero} is Mathlib's
517	      canonical projection to the additive value group with
518	      junk-on-zero: \texttt{log\_zero}: \(\log 0 = 0\);
519	      \texttt{log\_mul}: \(\log(x \cdot y) = \log x + \log y\) for
520	      \(x, y \neq 0\). This makes the downstream additive identities
521	      \(v_Y(fg) = v_Y(f) + v_Y(g)\), \(v_Y(f^{-1}) = -v_Y(f)\),
522	      \(v_Y(1) = 0\) available as named lemmas for
523	      \ref{def:order_at_point}.
524	  \end{itemize}
525	  \emph{Junk-on-\(f = 0\) convention.} Setting \(f = 0\) in
526	  \texttt{X.functionField} feeds \(0\) into
527	  \texttt{Ring.ordFrac (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)} (zero of the
528	  monoid-with-zero hom; \texttt{Ring.ordFrac} maps \(0\) to
529	  \(0 \in \mathbb Z^{m_0}\)); then \texttt{WithZero.log 0 = 0} so
530	  \(\texttt{order}\ Y\ 0 = 0\). This is the canonical Mathlib junk
531	  choice; no separate \(f \neq 0\) hypothesis is threaded on the
532	  signature.
533	  \emph{In-tree gap-fill (project-side; Mathlib upstream opportunity).}
534	  Mathlib (as of \texttt{b80f227}) has no direct bridge from the
535	  topological condition \(\texttt{Order.coheight}\ Y.\texttt{point} = 1\)
536	  on \texttt{X.carrier} to the algebraic condition
537	  \(\texttt{Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)}\) on the
538	  stalk. The missing fact (Stacks tag~\texttt{02IZ} / \texttt{005X}) is
539	  the topological identity ``coheight of \(x\) in the scheme's
540	  specialisation preorder equals the height of the prime ideal
541	  corresponding to \(x\) in any affine chart''; the algebraic step
542	  ``ideal height \(=\) stalk Krull dim'' is in Mathlib as
543	  \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}
544	  (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Height}) and
545	  \texttt{IsAffineOpen.isLocalization\_stalk}
546	  (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme}). The iter-175
547	  workaround is to thread the explicit instance
548	  \texttt{[Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (X.presheaf.stalk Y.point)]} on
549	  \texttt{order} (and downstream consumers); a future two-line
550	  Mathlib upstream PR pinning the coheight-height bridge would let
551	  the project drop the explicit argument in favour of synthesis from
552	  \texttt{coheight Y.point = 1} via the \texttt{PrimeDivisor} carrier.
553	\end{definition}
554	\section{Divisor of a closed point on a curve}
555	\begin{definition}
556	\leanok
557	[Divisor of a closed point on a curve]
558	  \label{def:divisor_closed_point}
559	  \uses{def:codim1_cycles}
560	  
561	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint}
562	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6 ("Divisors on Curves"), p.~137 (read from
563	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 154):
564	  % "A prime divisor is just a closed point, so an arbitrary divisor can
565	  % be written \(D = \sum n_i P_i\), where the \(P_i\) are closed points, and
566	  % \(n_i \in \mathbf Z\)."
567	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now we come to the study of divisors on curves. If \(X\)
568	  % is a nonsingular curve, then \(X\) satisfies the condition \((\ast)\) used
569	  % above, so we can talk about divisors on \(X\). A prime divisor is just
570	  % a closed point, so an arbitrary divisor can be written $D = \sum n_i
571	  % P_i\(, where the \)P_i\( are closed points, and \)n_i \in \mathbf Z$."
572	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, p.~137 (``Divisors on Curves'').}
573	  Let \(C\) be a smooth proper curve over a field \(k\) and let \(P \in C\) be a
574	  closed point. The \emph{Weil divisor associated to \(P\)} is the element
575	  \[
576	    [P] \;:=\; 1 \cdot P \;\in\; \mathrm{Div}(C)
577	  \]
578	  i.e.\ the prime divisor \(P\) with multiplicity one. The assignment is
579	  well-defined because the closed point \(P\) is automatically a codimension-\(1\)
580	  integral closed subscheme of the \(1\)-dimensional integral scheme \(C\).
581	  Conversely, every prime divisor on \(C\) is of this form, so an arbitrary
582	  divisor on \(C\) is a finite formal \(\mathbb Z\)-linear combination
583	  \(\sum n_i [P_i]\) of closed points.
584	  \paragraph{Lean signature scope (iter-174 pin).} The Lean signature
585	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint} accepts a
586	  scheme \texttt{C : Scheme.\{u\}}, a point \texttt{P : C}, and a
587	  hypothesis \texttt{(\_hP : IsClosed (\{P\} : Set C))}. It does
588	  \emph{not} typeclass-pin ``smooth proper curve over \(\bar k\)'' at the
589	  carrier level, and it does \emph{not} thread the
590	  one-dimensional-integral-scheme content into the declaration head.
591	  Instead the iter-174 encoding is \emph{junk-defined} outside the
592	  codim-\(1\) regime:
593	  \[
594	    \texttt{ofClosedPoint}\ P\ \_\!hP\ :=\
595	      \begin{cases}
596	        \texttt{Finsupp.single}\ \langle P, h\rangle\ 1 &
597	          \text{if } h : \texttt{Order.coheight}\ P = 1,\\
598	        0 & \text{otherwise.}
599	      \end{cases}
600	  \]
601	  On the off-branch --- where \texttt{P} is not a codimension-one point
602	  (e.g.\ \texttt{P} is the generic point of \texttt{C}, or a closed point
603	  of codimension \(\geq 2\) on a higher-dimensional scheme) ---
604	  \texttt{ofClosedPoint} returns the zero divisor \(0 \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\). This
605	  convention lets the Lean signature avoid threading
606	  \texttt{[OneDimensional C]} / \texttt{[IsIntegral C]} typeclass payload
607	  at the declaration head; the well-definedness consumer must instead
608	  invoke the bridge equation lemmas
609	  \ref{lem:ofClosedPoint_eq_single} and \ref{lem:ofClosedPoint_eq_zero}
610	  to expose the two branches at the point of use.
611	  On the intended regime (one-dimensional integral scheme \(C\), \(P\) a
612	  closed point), the chapter's mathematical statement
613	  \([P] := 1 \cdot P \in \mathrm{Div}(C)\) is recovered by combining
614	  \ref{def:divisor_closed_point} with the chain
615	  \(\texttt{IsClosed \{P\}} \Rightarrow \texttt{Order.coheight}\ P = 1\)
616	  that the prover threads inside the consumer (using the
617	  one-dimensional-integrality content).
618	\end{definition}
619	\begin{lemma}
620	\leanok
621	[Bridge equation: \texttt{ofClosedPoint} in the codim-\(1\) regime]
622	  \label{lem:ofClosedPoint_eq_single}
623	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint_eq_single}
624	  Let \(C : \texttt{Scheme.\{u\}}\) be a scheme and let \(P \in C\) be a
625	  point with \(\texttt{IsClosed}\,(\{P\} : \texttt{Set}\ C)\). If
626	  \(h : \texttt{Order.coheight}\ P = 1\) (the codimension-one witness
627	  promoting \(P\) to a \texttt{PrimeDivisor} via \ref{def:divisor_closed_point}),
628	  then
629	  \[
630	    \texttt{ofClosedPoint}\ P\ \_\!hP \;=\;
631	      \texttt{Finsupp.single}\ \langle P, h\rangle\ 1
632	      \;\in\; \mathrm{Div}(C).
633	  \]
634	\end{lemma}
635	\begin{proof}
636	  Unfolds the dependent-\texttt{if} in the definition of
637	  \texttt{ofClosedPoint} (\ref{def:divisor_closed_point}) on the
638	  positive branch using the hypothesis \(h\).
639	\end{proof}

{"file_path":"/home/archon/.elan/toolchains/leanprover--lean4---v4.30.0-rc2/src/lean/Lean/Elab/Declaration.lean","content":"/-

Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
Authors: Leonardo de Moura, Sebastian Ullrich

module

prelude
public import Lean.Elab.MutualDef
public import Lean.Elab.MutualInductive
import Lean.Compiler.ExternAttr

public section
namespace Lean.Elab.Command

open Meta

private def ensureValidNamespace (name : Name) : MacroM Unit := do
  match name with
  | .str p s =>
    if s == "_root_" then
      Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, `_root_` is a reserved namespace"
    ensureValidNamespace p
  | .num .. => Macro.throwError s!"invalid namespace `{name}`, it must not contain numeric parts"
  | .anonymous => return ()

private def setDeclIdName (declId : Syntax) (nameNew : Name) : Syntax :=
  let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore declId
  -- We should not update the name of `def _root_.` declarations
  assert! !(`_root_).isPrefixOf id
  let idStx := mkIdent nameNew |>.raw.setInfo declId.getHeadInfo
  if declId.isIdent then
    idStx
  else
    declId.setArg 0 idStx

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is a `Command.declaration`, and it is a declaration that always has a name. -/
private def isNamedDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  if !stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration then
    false
  else
    let decl := stx[1]
    let k := decl.getKind
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.abbrev ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.definition ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.theorem ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.opaque ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.axiom ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.inductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive ||
    k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.structure

/-- Return `true` if `stx` is an `instance` declaration command -/
private def isInstanceDecl (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx.isOfKind ``Lean.Parser.Command.declaration &&
  stx[1].getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.instance

/-- Return `some name` if `stx` is a declaration named `name` -/
private def getDeclName? (stx : Syntax) : Option Name := do
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore stx[1][1]
    some id
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    let optDeclId := stx[1][3]
    if optDeclId.isNone then none
    else
      let (id, _) := expandDeclIdCore optDeclId[0]
      some id
  else
    none

/--
Update the name of the given declaration.
This function assumes `stx` is not a nameless instance.
-/
private def setDeclName (stx : Syntax) (name : Name) : Syntax :=
  if isNamedDecl stx then
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 1 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][1] name
  else if isInstanceDecl stx then
    -- We never set the name of nameless instance declarations
    assert! !stx[1][3].isNone
    stx.setArg 1 <| stx[1].setArg 3 <| stx[1][3].setArg 0 <| setDeclIdName stx[1][3][0] name
  else
    stx

/--
  Given declarations such as `@[...] def Foo.Bla.f ...` return `some (Foo.Bla, @[...] def f ...)`
  Remark: if the id starts with `_root_`, we return `none`.
-/
private def expandDeclNamespace? (stx : Syntax) : MacroM (Option (Name × Syntax)) := do
  let some name := getDeclName? stx | return none
  if (`_root_).isPrefixOf name then
    ensureValidNamespace (name.replacePrefix `_root_ Name.anonymous)
    return none
  let scpView := extractMacroScopes name
  match scpView.name with
  | .str .anonymous _ => return none
  | .str pre shortName => return some (pre, setDeclName stx { scpView with name := .mkSimple shortName }.review)
  | _ => return none

def elabAxiom (modifiers : Modifiers) (stx : Syntax) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  -- leading_parser "axiom " >> declId >> declSig
  let declId             := stx[1]
  let (binders, typeStx) := expandDeclSig stx[2]
  runTermElabM fun vars => do
    let scopeLevelNames ← Term.getLevelNames
    let ⟨shortName, declName, allUserLevelNames, docString?⟩ ← Term.expandDeclId (← getCurrNamespace) scopeLevelNames declId modifiers
    addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin declName modifiers.stx stx
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicit do
    Term.withAutoBoundImplicitForbiddenPred (fun n => shortName == n) do
    Term.withDeclName declName <| Term.withLevelNames allUserLevelNames <| Term.elabBinders binders.getArgs fun xs => do
      Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.beforeElaboration
      let type ← Term.elabType typeStx
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      let xs ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (declId.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
      let type ← instantiateMVars type
      let type ← mkForallFVars xs type
      let type ← mkForallFVars vars type (usedOnly := true)
      let type ← Term.levelMVarToParam type
      let usedParams  := collectLevelParams {} type |>.params
      match sortDeclLevelParams scopeLevelNames allUserLevelNames usedParams with
      | Except.error msg      => throwErrorAt stx msg
      | Except.ok levelParams =>
        let type ← instantiateMVars type
        let decl := Declaration.axiomDecl {
          name        := declName,
          levelParams := levelParams,
          type        := type,
          isUnsafe    := modifiers.isUnsafe
        }
        trace[Elab.axiom] "{declName} : {type}"
        Term.ensureNoUnassignedMVars decl
        addDecl decl

        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterTypeChecking
        if isExtern (← getEnv) declName then
          compileDecl decl
        if let some (doc, isVerso) := docString? then
          addDocStringOf isVerso declName binders doc
        Term.applyAttributesAt declName modifiers.attrs AttributeApplicationTime.afterCompilation
        withSaveInfoContext do  -- save new env with docstring and decl
          Term.addTermInfo' declId (← mkConstWithLevelParams declName) (isBinder := true)
        enableRealizationsForConst declName
open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
/--
Macro that expands a declaration with a complex name into an explicit `namespace` block.
Implementing this step as a macro means that reuse checking is handled by `elabCommand`.
 -/
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.declaration]
def expandNamespacedDeclaration : Macro := fun stx => do
  match (← expandDeclNamespace? stx) with
  | some (ns, newStx) => do
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    let declTk := stx[1][0]
    let ns := mkIdentFrom declTk ns
    withRef declTk `(namespace $ns $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $(⟨newStx⟩) end $ns)
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab declaration, builtin_incremental]
def elabDeclaration : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  let modifiers : TSyntax ``Parser.Command.declModifiers := ⟨stx[0]⟩
  let decl     := stx[1]
  let declKind := decl.getKind
  if isDefLike decl then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef #[stx]
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    -- use hash of declaration name, if any, as stable quot context; `elabMutualDef` has its own
    -- handling
    withInitQuotContext (getDeclName? stx |>.map hash) do
    let mut modifiers ← elabModifiers modifiers
    if (← getScope).isMeta && modifiers.computeKind == .regular then
      modifiers := { modifiers with computeKind := .meta }
    withExporting (isExporting := modifiers.isInferredPublic (← getEnv)) do
      if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«axiom» then
        elabAxiom modifiers decl
      else if declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«inductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«coinductive»
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.classInductive
          || declKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.«structure» then
        elabInductive modifiers decl
      else
        throwError "unexpected declaration"

/-- Return true if all elements of the mutual-block are definitions/theorems/abbrevs. -/
private def isMutualDefLike (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  stx[1].getArgs.all fun elem =>
    let decl := elem[1]
    isDefLike decl

private def isMutualPreambleCommand (stx : Syntax) : Bool :=
  let k := stx.getKind
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.variable ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.universe ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.check ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.set_option ||
  k == ``Lean.Parser.Command.open

private partial def splitMutualPreamble (elems : Array Syntax) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
  let rec loop (i : Nat) : Option (Array Syntax × Array Syntax) :=
    if h : i < elems.size then
      if isMutualPreambleCommand elems[i] then
        loop (i+1)
      else if i == 0 then
        none -- `mutual` block does not contain any preamble commands
      else
        some (elems[*...i], elems[i...elems.size])
    else
      none -- a `mutual` block containing only preamble commands is not a valid `mutual` block
  loop 0

/--
Find the common namespace for the given names.
Example:
```
findCommonPrefix [`Lean.Elab.eval, `Lean.mkConst, `Lean.Elab.Tactic.evalTactic]
-- `Lean
```
-/
def findCommonPrefix (ns : List Name) : Name :=
  match ns with
  | [] => .anonymous
  | n :: ns => go n ns
where
  go (n : Name) (ns : List Name) : Name :=
    match n with
    | .anonymous => .anonymous
    | _ => match ns with
      | [] => n
      | n' :: ns => go (findCommon n.components n'.components) ns
  findCommon (as bs : List Name) : Name :=
    match as, bs with
    | a :: as, b :: bs => if a == b then a ++ findCommon as bs else .anonymous
    | _, _ => .anonymous


@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualNamespace : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut nss := #[]
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    match (← expandDeclNamespace? elem) with
    | none        => Macro.throwUnsupported
    | some (n, _) => nss := nss.push n
  let common := findCommonPrefix nss.toList
  if common.isAnonymous then Macro.throwUnsupported
  let elemsNew ← stx[1].getArgs.mapM fun elem => do
    let some name := getDeclName? elem | unreachable!
    let view := extractMacroScopes name
    let nameNew := { view with name := view.name.replacePrefix common .anonymous }.review
    return setDeclName elem nameNew
  let ns := mkIdentFrom stx common
  let stxNew := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  `(namespace $ns $(⟨stxNew⟩) end $ns)

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualElement : Macro := fun stx => do
  let mut elemsNew := #[]
  let mut modified := false
  for elem in stx[1].getArgs do
    -- Don't trigger the `expandNamespacedDecl` macro, the namespace is handled by the mutual def
    -- elaborator directly instead
    if !elem.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.declaration then
      if let some elemNew ← expandMacro? elem then
        if elemNew.isOfKind nullKind then
          elemsNew := elemsNew ++ elemNew.getArgs
        else
          elemsNew := elemsNew.push elemNew
        modified := true
        continue
    elemsNew := elemsNew.push elem
  if modified then
    return stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode elemsNew)
  else
    Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.mutual]
def expandMutualPreamble : Macro := fun stx =>
  match splitMutualPreamble stx[1].getArgs with
  | none => Macro.throwUnsupported
  | some (preamble, rest) => do
    let secCmd    ← `(section)
    let newMutual := stx.setArg 1 (mkNullNode rest)
    let endCmd    ← `(end)
    return mkNullNode (#[secCmd] ++ preamble ++ #[newMutual] ++ #[endCmd])

@[builtin_command_elab «mutual», builtin_incremental]
def elabMutual : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  withExporting (isExporting := (← getScope).isPublic) do
  if isMutualDefLike stx then
    -- only case implementing incrementality currently
    elabMutualDef stx[1].getArgs
  else withoutCommandIncrementality true do
    if ← isMutualInductive stx then
      elabMutualInductive stx[1].getArgs
    else
      throwError "invalid mutual block: either all elements of the block must be inductive/structure declarations, or they must all be definitions/theorems/abbrevs"

/- leading_parser "attribute " >> "[" >> sepBy1 (eraseAttr <|> Term.attrInstance) ", " >> "]" >> many1 ident -/
@[builtin_command_elab «attribute»] def elabAttr : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let mut attrInsts := #[]
  let mut toErase := #[]
  for attrKindStx in stx[2].getSepArgs do
    if attrKindStx.getKind == ``Lean.Parser.Command.eraseAttr then
      let attrName := attrKindStx[1].getId.eraseMacroScopes
      if isAttribute (← getEnv) attrName then
        toErase := toErase.push attrName
      else
        logErrorAt attrKindStx m!"unknown attribute [{attrName}]"
    else
      attrInsts := attrInsts.push attrKindStx
  let attrs ← elabAttrs attrInsts
  let idents := stx[4].getArgs
  for ident in idents do withRef ident <| liftTermElabM do
    /-
    HACK to allow `attribute` command to disable builtin simprocs.
    TODO: find a better solution. Example: have some "fake" declaration
    for builtin simprocs.
    -/
    let declNames ←
      try
        realizeGlobalConstWithInfos ident
      catch _ =>
        let name := ident.getId.eraseMacroScopes
        if (← Simp.isBuiltinSimproc name) then
          pure [name]
        else
          throwUnknownConstantAt ident name
    let declName ← ensureNonAmbiguous ident declNames
    withExporting (isExporting := !isPrivateName declName && attrs.any (·.kind != .local)) do
      recordExtraModUseFromDecl (isMeta := false) declName
    Term.applyAttributes declName attrs
    for attrName in toErase do
      Attribute.erase declName attrName
    if (← getEnv).isImportedConst declName then
      if let some attr := attrs.find? (·.kind != .local) then
        -- If an imported declaration is marked with a non-local attribute, Shake must be informed
        -- about this indirect reference
        recordIndirectModUse attr.name.toString declName

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.«initialize»] def elabInitialize : CommandElab
  | stx@`($declModifiers:declModifiers $kw:initializeKeyword $[$id? : $type? ←]? $doSeq) => do
    let attrId := mkIdentFrom stx <| if kw.raw[0].isToken "initialize" then `init else `builtin_init
    if let (some id, some type) := (id?, type?) then
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := stx[0]
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let defStx ← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$attrId:ident initFn, $(attrs?.getD ∅),*] $(vis?)? $[meta%$meta?]? opaque $id : $type)
      let mut fullId := (← getCurrNamespace) ++ id.getId
      if vis?.any (·.raw.isOfKind ``Parser.Command.private) then
        fullId := mkPrivateName (← getEnv) fullId
      -- We need to add `id`'s ranges *before* elaborating `initFn` (and then `id` itself) as
      -- otherwise the info context created by `with_decl_name` will be incomplete and break the
      -- call hierarchy
      addDeclarationRangesForBuiltin fullId ⟨defStx.raw[0]⟩ defStx.raw[1]
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (!isPrivateName fullId)
      elabCommand (← `(
        $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO $type := with_decl_name% $(mkIdent fullId) do $doSeq
        $defStx:command))
    else
      let `(Parser.Command.declModifiersT| $[$doc?:docComment]? $[@[$attrs?,*]]? $(_)? $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]?) := declModifiers
        | throwErrorAt declModifiers "invalid initialization command, unexpected modifiers"
      let attrs := (attrs?.map (·.getElems)).getD #[]
      let attrs := attrs.push (← `(Lean.Parser.Term.attrInstance| $attrId:ident))
      -- `[builtin_init]` can be private as it is used for local codegen only but `[init]` must be
      -- available for the interpreter.
      let vis := Parser.Command.visibility.ofBool (attrId.getId == `init)
      elabCommand (← `($[$doc?:docComment]? @[$[$attrs],*] $vis:visibility $[meta%$meta?]? $[unsafe%$unsafe?]? def initFn : IO Unit := do $doSeq))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

builtin_initialize
  registerTraceClass `Elab.axiom

end Lean.Elab.Command
"}

231	Note that we cannot just use `fun x ↦ ord R x` without further assumptions on `R`.

238	  toFun x := if x ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
239	             then (ENat.recTopCoe 0 (WithZero.coe <| Multiplicative.ofAdd ·) (Ring.ord R x))

243	  map_mul' := by

246	    · rw [ord_mul _ b]
247	      generalize ord R x = x'
248	      generalize ord R y = y'
249	      cases x' <;> cases y'

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Raphael Douglas Giles. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Raphael Douglas Giles
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.NonZeroDivisors
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Length
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.HopkinsLevitzki
11	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Ring.Hom.InjSurj
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.Quotient.Noetherian
13	
14	/-!
15	# Order of vanishing
16	
17	This file defines the order of vanishing of an element of a ring as the length of the quotient of
18	the ring by the ideal generated by that element. We also define the extension of this notion to the
19	field of fractions
20	-/
21	@[expose] public section
22	
23	open LinearMap Pointwise IsLocalization Ideal WithZero
24	
25	variable {R : Type*} {M : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M]
26	
27	namespace Ring
28	
29	variable (R) [Ring R]
30	/--
31	Order of vanishing function for elements of a ring.
32	-/
33	@[stacks 02MD]
34	noncomputable
35	def ord (x : R) : ℕ∞ := Module.length R (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x})
36	
37	@[simp]
38	lemma ord_one : ord R 1 = 0 := by
39	  simp_all [ord,
40	    Ideal.span_singleton_one, Submodule.Quotient.subsingleton_iff]
41	
42	end Ring
43	
44	variable [CommRing R] [Module R M]
45	
46	/--
47	The map `R ⧸ I →ₗ[R] R ⧸ (a • I)` defined by multiplication by `a`
48	-/
49	def Ideal.mulQuot (a : R) (I : Ideal R) :
50	    R ⧸ I →ₗ[R] R ⧸ (a • I) :=
51	  Submodule.mapQ _ _ (LinearMap.mul R R a) (Submodule.le_comap_map _ _)
52	
53	/--
54	The map `R ⧸ I →ₗ[R] R ⧸ (a • I)` defined by multiplication by `a` is injective if `a` is
55	a nonzero divisor.
56	-/
57	lemma Ideal.mulQuot_injective {a : R} (I : Ideal R) (ha : a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R) :
58	    Function.Injective (Ideal.mulQuot a I) := by
59	  simp only [mulQuot, Submodule.mapQ, ← ker_eq_bot]
60	  apply Submodule.ker_liftQ_eq_bot'
61	  apply le_antisymm
62	  · have : Submodule.map (mul R R a) I = a • I := rfl
63	    rw [le_ker_iff_map, Submodule.map_comp, this, Submodule.mkQ_map_self]
64	  · have m : I = Submodule.comap (mul R R a) (a • I) := by
65	      ext b
66	      exact (Submodule.mul_mem_smul_iff ha).symm
67	    simp [← m, ker_comp]
68	
69	/--
70	The quotient map `(R ⧸ a • I) →ₗ[R] (R ⧸ Ideal.span {a})`.
71	-/
72	def Ideal.quotOfMul (a : R) (I : Ideal R) :
73	    (R ⧸ a • I) →ₗ[R] (R ⧸ Ideal.span {a}) :=
74	  Submodule.factor <| Submodule.singleton_set_smul I a ▸ Submodule.smul_le_span {a} I
75	
76	/--
77	The quotient map `(R ⧸ a • I) →ₗ[R] (R ⧸ Ideal.span {a})` is surjective.
78	-/
79	lemma Ideal.quotOfMul_surjective {a : R} (I : Ideal R) :
80	    Function.Surjective (Ideal.quotOfMul a I) := by
81	  simp only [Ideal.quotOfMul]
82	  exact Submodule.factor_surjective <|
83	    Submodule.singleton_set_smul I a ▸ Submodule.smul_le_span {a} I
84	
85	/--
86	The sequence `R ⧸ I →ₗ[R] R ⧸ (a • I) →ₗ[R] R ⧸ (Ideal.span {a})` given by multiplication
87	by `a` then quotienting by the ideal generated by `a` is exact.
88	-/
89	lemma Ideal.exact_mulQuot_quotOfMul {a : R} (I : Ideal R) :
90	    Function.Exact (Ideal.mulQuot a I) (Ideal.quotOfMul a I) := by
91	  simp only [exact_iff]
92	  have : ker (Ideal.quotOfMul a I) = a • ⊤ := by
93	    simp only [← submodule_span_eq, quotOfMul, Submodule.factor, Submodule.mapQ, comp_id,
94	      Submodule.ker_liftQ, Submodule.ker_mkQ, Submodule.map_span, Submodule.mkQ_apply,
95	      Quotient.mk_eq_mk, Set.image_singleton, Quotient.smul_top]
96	  simp [this, Ideal.mulQuot, Submodule.mapQ.eq_1, Submodule.range_liftQ,
97	    range_comp, Ideal.Quotient.smul_top, ← Ideal.submodule_span_eq, LinearMap.map_span]
98	
99	namespace Ring
100	variable (R)
101	/--
102	The order of vanishing of `a * b` is the order of vanishing of `a` plus the order
103	of vanishing of `b`.
104	-/
105	theorem ord_mul {a b : R} (hb : b ∈ nonZeroDivisors R) :
106	    ord R (a * b) = ord R a + ord R b := by
107	  have := Module.length_eq_add_of_exact (Ideal.mulQuot b (Ideal.span {a}))
108	          (Ideal.quotOfMul b (Ideal.span {a})) (Ideal.mulQuot_injective (Ideal.span {a}) hb)
109	          (Ideal.quotOfMul_surjective (Ideal.span {a}))
110	          (Ideal.exact_mulQuot_quotOfMul (Ideal.span {a}))
111	  simp only [Ring.ord, ← this]
112	  have lem : (({b} : Set R) • Ideal.span {a}) = Ideal.span {b * a} := by
113	    simp [← Ideal.submodule_span_eq, Submodule.set_smul_span]
114	  have : (({b} : Set R) • Ideal.span {a}) = b • Ideal.span {a} := Submodule.singleton_set_smul
115	    (Ideal.span {a}) b
116	  rw [this] at lem
117	  rw [lem, mul_comm]
118	
119	/--
120	Variation of `ord_mul` where the user has to show the first input is a non
121	zero divisor rather than the second.
122	-/
123	lemma ord_mul' {a b : R} (ha : a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R) :
124	    ord R (a * b) = ord R a + ord R b := by
125	  rw [mul_comm, ord_mul R ha, add_comm]
126	
127	/--
128	The order of zero is `Module.length R R`. Use this when it is necessary to unfold the definition
129	of `ord` to avoid annoyances of working with `R ⧸ Ideal.span {0}` instead of `R`.
130	-/
131	lemma ord_zero : ord R 0 = Module.length R R := by
132	  simp only [ord]
133	  let m := (Submodule.quotEquivOfEqBot (Ideal.span {0} : Submodule R R) (span_singleton_zero))
134	  exact le_antisymm (Module.length_le_of_injective m m.injective)
135	      (Module.length_le_of_surjective m m.surjective)
136	
137	variable {R}
138	/--
139	For `x : R` a non zero divisor, `ord R (x ^ n) = n • ord R x`.
140	-/
141	@[simp]
142	theorem ord_pow {x : R} (hx : x ∈ nonZeroDivisors R) (n : ℕ) : ord R (x ^ n) = n • ord R x := by
143	  induction n with
144	  | zero => simp
145	  | succ n ih =>
146	    rw [pow_succ, ord_mul, ih, succ_nsmul]
147	    exact hx
148	
149	@[simp]
150	lemma ord_mul_of_isUnit_left {a : R} (h : IsUnit a) (x : R) : ord R (a * x) = ord R x := by
151	  rw [ord, ord, Ideal.span_singleton_mul_left_unit h x]
152	
153	@[simp]
154	lemma ord_mul_of_isUnit_right {a : R} (h : IsUnit a) (x : R) : ord R (x * a) = ord R x := by
155	  rw [ord, ord, Ideal.span_singleton_mul_right_unit h x]
156	
157	lemma ord_eq_of_associated {x y : R} (h : Associated x y) : ord R x = ord R y := by
158	  obtain ⟨a, rfl⟩ := h
159	  simp
160	
161	@[simp]
162	lemma ord_neg (x : R) : ord R (-x) = ord R x:= by
163	    simp [ord_eq_of_associated (x := -x) (y := x) (by simp)]
164	
165	/--
166	In an `S` algebra `R`, the order of vanishing of `x : R` is equal to the order of vanishing
167	of `a • x` for `a` a unit in `S`.
168	-/
169	@[simp]
170	lemma ord_smul_of_isUnit {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra S R]
171	    {a : S} (h : IsUnit a) (x : R) : ord R (a • x) = ord R x := by
172	  rw [Algebra.smul_def a x]
173	  exact ord_mul_of_isUnit_left (RingHom.isUnit_map (algebraMap S R) h) x
174	
175	/-
176	Note that the order here is the order on `ℕ∞` where `∞` is a top element, rather than the order on
177	`ℤᵐ⁰` which also comes up when working with orders of vanishing.
178	-/
179	lemma ord_le_ord_mul (a : R) (x : R) : ord R x ≤ ord R (a * x) := by
180	  simp only [ord]
181	  suffices Ideal.span {a * x} ≤ Ideal.span {x} by
182	    let g : (R ⧸ Ideal.span {a * x}) →ₗ[R] (R ⧸ Ideal.span {x}) := Submodule.factor this
183	    refine Module.length_le_of_surjective (Submodule.factor this) (Submodule.factor_surjective this)
184	  rw [Ideal.span_singleton_le_span_singleton]
185	  exact Dvd.intro_left (Algebra.algebraMap a) rfl
186	
187	lemma ord_le_ord_of_dvd {a x : R} (h : a ∣ x) : ord R a ≤ ord R x := by
188	  obtain ⟨b, rfl⟩ := h
189	  rw [mul_comm]
190	  exact ord_le_ord_mul b a
191	
192	/--
193	In an `S` algebra `R`, the order of vanishing of `x : R` is less than or equal
194	to the order of vanishing of `a • x` for any `a : S`. One should note that the order here
195	is the order on `ℕ∞` where `∞` is a top element.
196	-/
197	lemma ord_le_smul {S : Type*} [CommRing S] [Algebra S R] (a : S) (x : R) :
198	    ord R x ≤ ord R (a • x) := by simp [Algebra.smul_def, ord_le_ord_mul]

import Mathlib

open Ring

example {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
    [IsNoetherianRing R] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 R]
    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [IsNoetherianRing S] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) :
    Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  -- IsScalarTower
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x}) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    induction m using Submodule.Quotient.induction_on with
    | h y =>
      change _ = _
      rfl
  -- Construct R-linear equivalence R/(x) ≃ₗ[R] S/(e x)
  -- via Ideal.quotientEquiv
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]
  let φ_ring : R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) ≃+* S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  -- Promote to R-linear equiv: structure transport.
  let φ : R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R) ≃ₗ[R] S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) := {
    φ_ring.toAddEquiv with
    map_smul' := fun r m => by
      induction m using Submodule.Quotient.induction_on with
      | h y =>
        change φ_ring (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (r * y)) = (algebraMap R S r) • (φ_ring (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ y))
        rw [Algebra.smul_def]
        change φ_ring (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (r * y)) = (algebraMap R S r) * φ_ring (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ y)
        rw [map_mul]
        rfl
  }
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  -- Now both sides are over the same R, transporting to S
  -- Apply Module.length_eq_of_surjective with R-side = R, S-side = S
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x}) e.surjective

example {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]

  letI : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  -- IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    show (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
    rw [mul_smul]
  -- Construct the ring iso
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]
  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  -- Promote to R-linear equiv
  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
    intro r m
    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
    | h y =>
      show φ_ring _ = (algebraMap R S r) • (φ_ring _)
      sorry
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x}) e.surjective

import Mathlib

open Ring

namespace Ring

private lemma ord_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) : Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    change (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
    rw [mul_smul]
  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
    intro r m
    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
    | h y =>
      change Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e (r * y)) = e r • Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y)
      rw [map_mul]; rfl
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
    e.surjective

private lemma nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R ↔ e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := by
  constructor
  · intro hr s hs
    have hsr : (e.symm s) * r = 0 := by
      apply e.injective
      rw [map_mul, map_zero, RingEquiv.apply_symm_apply, hs]
    have := hr _ hsr
    rw [← e.apply_symm_apply s, this, map_zero]
  · intro hr s hs
    have : e (s * r) = 0 := by rw [map_mul, hs, mul_zero]
    apply e.injective
    rw [map_zero]
    have := hr _ this
    exact this

private lemma ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r := by
  unfold Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom
  simp only [MonoidWithZeroHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
  by_cases hr : r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
  · have her : e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mp hr
    simp [hr, her, ord_ringEquiv e r]
  · have her : e r ∉ nonZeroDivisors S := fun h =>
      hr ((nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mpr h)
    simp [hr, her]

end Ring

import Mathlib

example {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :

import Mathlib

open Ring

namespace Ring

private lemma ord_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) : Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    change (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
    rw [mul_smul]
  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
    intro r m
    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
    | h y =>
      change Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e (r * y)) = e r • Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y)
      rw [map_mul]; rfl
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
    e.surjective

private lemma nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R ↔ e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := by
  rw [← MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors (e : R ≃* S)]
  refine ⟨fun h => ⟨r, h, rfl⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨r', hr', heq⟩ := h
  have : r = r' := e.injective heq.symm
  rw [this]; exact hr'

private lemma ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r := by
  unfold Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom
  simp only [MonoidWithZeroHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
  by_cases hr : r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
  · have her : e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mp hr
    simp [hr, her, ord_ringEquiv e r]
  · have her : e r ∉ nonZeroDivisors S := fun h =>
      hr ((nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mpr h)
    simp [hr, her]

end Ring

import Mathlib

open Ring

namespace Ring

private lemma ord_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) : Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    change (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
    rw [mul_smul]
  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
    intro r m
    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
    | h y =>
      change Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e (r * y)) = e r • Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y)
      rw [map_mul]; rfl
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
    e.surjective

private lemma nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R ↔ e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := by
  rw [← MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors (e : R ≃* S)]
  refine ⟨fun h => ⟨r, h, rfl⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨r', hr', heq⟩ := h
  have : r = r' := e.injective heq.symm
  rw [this]; exact hr'

private lemma ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r := by
  unfold Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom
  simp only [MonoidWithZeroHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
  by_cases hr : r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
  · have her : e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mp hr
    simp [hr, her, ord_ringEquiv e r]
  · have her : e r ∉ nonZeroDivisors S := fun h =>
      hr ((nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mpr h)
    simp [hr, her]

private lemma ordFrac_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R]
    [IsNoetherianRing R] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 R]
    [CommRing S] [IsDomain S] [IsNoetherianRing S] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 S]
    {K_R K_S : Type*} [Field K_R] [Field K_S]
    [Algebra R K_R] [IsFractionRing R K_R]
    [Algebra S K_S] [IsFractionRing S K_S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (e_K : K_R ≃+* K_S)
    (h_compat : ∀ r : R, e_K (algebraMap R K_R r) = algebraMap S K_S (e r))
    (x : K_R) :
    Ring.ordFrac S (e_K x) = Ring.ordFrac R x := by
  by_cases hx : x = 0
  · subst hx; rw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
  obtain ⟨⟨a, b⟩, hab⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (nonZeroDivisors R) x
  have hb_nzd : (b : R) ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := b.2
  have h_inj_R : Function.Injective (algebraMap R K_R) := IsFractionRing.injective R K_R
  have hb_K_ne : algebraMap R K_R (b : R) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [Ne, ← map_zero (algebraMap R K_R), h_inj_R.eq_iff,
        ← mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_ne_zero]
    exact hb_nzd
  have ha_ne : a ≠ 0 := by
    intro ha
    subst ha
    rw [map_zero] at hab
    rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hab with hx' | hb_ne
    · exact hx hx'
    · exact hb_K_ne hb_ne
  have ha_nzd : a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero ha_ne
  -- e b ∈ nzd S, e a ∈ nzd S
  have he_b_nzd : e b ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e b).mp hb_nzd
  have he_a_nzd : e a ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e a).mp ha_nzd
  -- x = mk' K_R a ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩
  have hx_mk : x = IsLocalization.mk' K_R a ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩ := by
    rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul, hab]
  -- e_K x = mk' K_S (e a) ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩
  have he_K_x : e_K x = IsLocalization.mk' K_S (e a) ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩ := by
    rw [hx_mk]
    -- e_K (mk' K_R a b) = mk' K_S (e a) (e b)
    apply (IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul (S := K_S)).mpr
    rw [← h_compat, ← h_compat]
    rw [← map_mul]
    congr 1
    rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul]
  rw [he_K_x, hx_mk]
  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div S ⟨e a, he_a_nzd⟩ ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩]
  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div R ⟨a, ha_nzd⟩ ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩]
  rw [ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e a, ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e b]

end Ring

import Mathlib

open Ring

namespace Ring

private lemma ord_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) : Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    change (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
    rw [mul_smul]
  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
    intro r m
    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
    | h y =>
      change Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e (r * y)) = e r • Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y)
      rw [map_mul]; rfl
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
    e.surjective

private lemma nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R ↔ e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := by
  rw [← MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors (e : R ≃* S)]
  refine ⟨fun h => ⟨r, h, rfl⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨r', hr', heq⟩ := h
  have : r = r' := e.injective heq.symm
  rw [this]; exact hr'

private lemma ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r := by
  unfold Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom
  simp only [MonoidWithZeroHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
  by_cases hr : r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
  · have her : e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mp hr
    simp [hr, her, ord_ringEquiv e r]
  · have her : e r ∉ nonZeroDivisors S := fun h =>
      hr ((nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mpr h)
    simp [hr, her]

private lemma ordFrac_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R]
    [IsNoetherianRing R] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 R]
    [CommRing S] [IsDomain S] [IsNoetherianRing S] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 S]
    {K_R K_S : Type*} [Field K_R] [Field K_S]
    [Algebra R K_R] [IsFractionRing R K_R]
    [Algebra S K_S] [IsFractionRing S K_S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (e_K : K_R ≃+* K_S)
    (h_compat : ∀ r : R, e_K (algebraMap R K_R r) = algebraMap S K_S (e r))
    (x : K_R) :
    Ring.ordFrac S (e_K x) = Ring.ordFrac R x := by
  by_cases hx : x = 0
  · subst hx; rw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
  obtain ⟨⟨a, b⟩, hab⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (nonZeroDivisors R) x
  have hb_nzd : (b : R) ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := b.2
  have hb_ne : (b : R) ≠ 0 := mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_ne_zero.mp hb_nzd
  have h_inj_R : Function.Injective (algebraMap R K_R) := IsFractionRing.injective R K_R
  have hb_K_ne : algebraMap R K_R (b : R) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [Ne, ← map_zero (algebraMap R K_R), h_inj_R.eq_iff]
    exact hb_ne
  have ha_ne : a ≠ 0 := by
    intro ha
    subst ha
    rw [map_zero] at hab
    rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hab with hx' | hb_ne'
    · exact hx hx'
    · exact hb_K_ne hb_ne'
  have ha_nzd : a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero ha_ne
  have he_b_nzd : e b ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e b).mp hb_nzd
  have he_a_nzd : e a ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e a).mp ha_nzd
  have hx_mk : x = IsLocalization.mk' K_R a ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩ := by
    rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul, hab]
  have he_K_x : e_K x = IsLocalization.mk' K_S (e a) ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩ := by
    rw [eq_comm]
    refine (IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul).mpr ?_
    rw [← h_compat a, ← h_compat b, ← map_mul]
    exact congrArg e_K hab.symm
  rw [he_K_x, hx_mk]
  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div S ⟨e a, he_a_nzd⟩ ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩]
  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div R ⟨a, ha_nzd⟩ ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩]
  congr 1
  · exact ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e a
  · exact ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e b

end Ring

37	noncomputable abbrev Scheme.functionField [IrreducibleSpace X] : CommRingCat :=

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `Ring.ordFrac` naturality

Iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 (HARD BAR + extras). Naturality of
`Ring.ord`, `Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom`, and `Ring.ordFrac` (Mathlib's
`K →*₀ ℤᵐ⁰` from `Mathlib.RingTheory.OrderOfVanishing.Basic`) across a ring
isomorphism `e : R ≃+* S` between commutative rings, lifted to a compatible
fraction-field isomorphism `e_K : Frac R ≃+* Frac S`.

This is the algebraic substrate consumed by iter-202+ Sub-build 3
(scheme-level `Scheme.RationalMap.order` naturality across the iter-200
`Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso` open-immersion bridge), which in turn closes
the non-zero branch of `rationalMap_order_finite_support` (the L535 sorry).

References: Stacks 02RV (Hartshorne II.6.1), Stacks 02ME (DVR-of-stalk
characterisation at codim-1 points), Stacks 02IZ (stalk under open
immersion), Stacks 02MD (Mathlib's `Ring.ordFrac` / `Ring.ord` API).
-/

namespace Ring

/-- **`Ring.ord` naturality under a ring isomorphism.**
`ord R x = ord S (e x)`. The length of `R/(x)` as `R`-module equals the
length of `S/(e x)` as `S`-module: the `R`-linear equivalence
`R/(x) ≃ₗ[R] S/(e x)` (with the `S`-side equipped with `R`-module structure
via `e`) transports length across rings using `Module.length_eq_of_surjective`.

Project-local because Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `ord` naturality lemma. -/
private lemma ord_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (x : R) : Ring.ord R x = Ring.ord S (e x) := by
  have heq : Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S) = Ideal.map (e : R →+* S) (Ideal.span {x}) := by
    rw [Ideal.map_span, Set.image_singleton]; rfl
  letI alg : Algebra R S := e.toRingHom.toAlgebra
  haveI : IsScalarTower R S (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine ⟨fun r s m => ?_⟩
    change (algebraMap R S r * s) • m = (algebraMap R S r) • (s • m)
    rw [mul_smul]
  let φ_ring : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃+* (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) :=
    Ideal.quotientEquiv (Ideal.span {x}) (Ideal.span {e x}) e heq
  let φ : (R ⧸ Ideal.span ({x} : Set R)) ≃ₗ[R] (S ⧸ Ideal.span ({e x} : Set S)) := by
    refine { φ_ring.toAddEquiv with map_smul' := ?_ }
    intro r m
    induction m using Quotient.inductionOn with
    | h y =>
      change Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e (r * y)) = e r • Ideal.Quotient.mk _ (e y)
      rw [map_mul]; rfl
  unfold Ring.ord
  rw [φ.length_eq]
  exact Module.length_eq_of_surjective (S := R) (R := S) (M := S ⧸ Ideal.span {e x})
    e.surjective

/-- **`nonZeroDivisors` is preserved by a ring isomorphism.** Direct from
Mathlib's `MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors`. Project-local convenience
wrapper to phrase the result as an `Iff` instead of a `Submonoid.map` equality. -/
private lemma nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R ↔ e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := by
  rw [← MulEquivClass.map_nonZeroDivisors (e : R ≃* S)]
  refine ⟨fun h => ⟨r, h, rfl⟩, fun h => ?_⟩
  obtain ⟨r', hr', heq⟩ := h
  have : r = r' := e.injective heq.symm
  rw [this]; exact hr'

/-- **`Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom` naturality under a ring isomorphism.**
`ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r`. Reduces to
`ord_ringEquiv` after splitting on `r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R`. -/
private lemma ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [Nontrivial R]
    [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] (e : R ≃+* S) (r : R) :
    Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom S (e r) = Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom R r := by
  unfold Ring.ordMonoidWithZeroHom
  simp only [MonoidWithZeroHom.coe_mk, ZeroHom.coe_mk]
  by_cases hr : r ∈ nonZeroDivisors R
  · have her : e r ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mp hr
    simp [hr, her, ord_ringEquiv e r]
  · have her : e r ∉ nonZeroDivisors S := fun h =>
      hr ((nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e r).mpr h)
    simp [hr, her]

/-- **`Ring.ordFrac` naturality** — iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 HARD BAR
(per PROGRESS.md L108--L122 recipe step (1)).

Given a ring iso `e : R ≃+* S` between Noetherian Krull-dim-`≤ 1` integral
domains and a compatible fraction-field iso `e_K : K_R ≃+* K_S` (compatibility
`e_K (algebraMap R K_R r) = algebraMap S K_S (e r)` for `r : R`), the
order-of-vanishing function on the fraction fields is invariant:
`ordFrac S (e_K x) = ordFrac R x` for any `x : K_R`.

Proof: `x = 0` is the junk-zero branch. For `x ≠ 0`, write `x = mk' K_R a b`
with `a, b ∈ nonZeroDivisors R` via `IsLocalization.surj`. Then
`e_K x = mk' K_S (e a) (e b)` via the compatibility hypothesis, and both
sides reduce via Mathlib's `ordFrac_eq_div` to a quotient of
`ordMonoidWithZeroHom`-values, which agree by `ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv`. -/
private lemma ordFrac_ringEquiv {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R]
    [IsNoetherianRing R] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 R]
    [CommRing S] [IsDomain S] [IsNoetherianRing S] [Ring.KrullDimLE 1 S]
    {K_R K_S : Type*} [Field K_R] [Field K_S]
    [Algebra R K_R] [IsFractionRing R K_R]
    [Algebra S K_S] [IsFractionRing S K_S]
    (e : R ≃+* S) (e_K : K_R ≃+* K_S)
    (h_compat : ∀ r : R, e_K (algebraMap R K_R r) = algebraMap S K_S (e r))
    (x : K_R) :
    Ring.ordFrac S (e_K x) = Ring.ordFrac R x := by
  by_cases hx : x = 0
  · subst hx; rw [map_zero, map_zero, map_zero]
  obtain ⟨⟨a, b⟩, hab⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (nonZeroDivisors R) x
  have hb_nzd : (b : R) ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := b.2
  have hb_ne : (b : R) ≠ 0 := mem_nonZeroDivisors_iff_ne_zero.mp hb_nzd
  have h_inj_R : Function.Injective (algebraMap R K_R) := IsFractionRing.injective R K_R
  have hb_K_ne : algebraMap R K_R (b : R) ≠ 0 := by
    rw [Ne, ← map_zero (algebraMap R K_R), h_inj_R.eq_iff]
    exact hb_ne
  have ha_ne : a ≠ 0 := by
    intro ha
    subst ha
    rw [map_zero] at hab
    rcases mul_eq_zero.mp hab with hx' | hb_ne'
    · exact hx hx'
    · exact hb_K_ne hb_ne'
  have ha_nzd : a ∈ nonZeroDivisors R := mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero ha_ne
  have he_b_nzd : e b ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e b).mp hb_nzd
  have he_a_nzd : e a ∈ nonZeroDivisors S := (nonZeroDivisors_ringEquiv e a).mp ha_nzd
  have hx_mk : x = IsLocalization.mk' K_R a ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩ := by
    rw [eq_comm, IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul, hab]
  have he_K_x : e_K x = IsLocalization.mk' K_S (e a) ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩ := by
    rw [eq_comm]
    refine (IsLocalization.mk'_eq_iff_eq_mul).mpr ?_
    rw [← h_compat a, ← h_compat b, ← map_mul]
    exact congrArg e_K hab.symm
  rw [he_K_x, hx_mk]
  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div S ⟨e a, he_a_nzd⟩ ⟨e b, he_b_nzd⟩]
  rw [Ring.ordFrac_eq_div R ⟨a, ha_nzd⟩ ⟨b, hb_nzd⟩]
  congr 1
  · exact ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e a
  · exact ordMonoidWithZeroHom_ringEquiv e b

end Ring

/-- **Function field isomorphism along an open immersion** (iter-201 Lane
WD-A4a Sub-build 2 PUSH-BEYOND). For an integral scheme `X` and a nonempty
open subscheme `U ⊆ X`, the function fields of `U.toScheme` and `X` are
canonically isomorphic. -/
noncomputable def Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso {X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral X]
    (U : X.Opens) [Nonempty U] :
    (U.toScheme).functionField ≅ X.functionField :=
  (Scheme.Opens.stalkIso U (genericPoint U.toScheme)).trans
    (X.presheaf.stalkCongr (by
      have heq : ((genericPoint U.toScheme : U.toScheme.carrier).val : X.carrier) =
          genericPoint X :=
        genericPoint_eq_of_isOpenImmersion U.ι
      rw [heq]
      exact Inseparable.refl _))

/-- **Scheme-side packaging of `Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv` for the open-immersion
PrimeDivisor stalk iso** (iter-201 Lane WD-A4a Sub-build 2 PUSH-BEYOND
packaging).

This is the iter-202+ Sub-build 3 entry point: it pipes the iter-200
`Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU` ring iso plus a user-supplied
function-field iso `e_K` (typically the `Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U`
above) and a compatibility hypothesis `h_compat` into the algebraic naturality
`Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv`.

The full Sub-build 3 closure replaces `e_K` with `Scheme.Opens.functionFieldIso U`
and discharges `h_compat` from the naturality of `Scheme.Opens.stalkIso` w.r.t.
`stalkSpecializes` to the generic point. That naturality lemma is iter-202
scope (it requires either a `Scheme.Hom.stalkSpecializes_stalkMap` analogue or a
direct germ-universal chase via Mathlib's
`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Opens.germ_stalkIso_hom_assoc`). -/
private theorem Scheme.PrimeDivisor.ordFrac_stalkIso_naturality

    (f : U.toScheme.functionField) :

        (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.restrictToOpen U Y hYU).point) f :=
  Ring.ordFrac_ringEquiv
    (Scheme.PrimeDivisor.stalkIso U Y hYU).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
    e_K h_compat f
